betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Designing & Recycling an RBMK§f

§aFrom beginner-friendly layouts to spent fuel reprocessing — master the full RBMK lifecycle!§f

§6Design Philosophy§f:
The goal is maximum power generation while preventing meltdown. Heat is generated by fuel rods and removed by steam channels. Steam channels should go near fuel rods (ideally diagonally). Neutrons travel horizontally — use moderators between rods that produce fast neutrons and rods that need slow neutrons. Seal the reactor with cover panels to prevent radiation leaks.

§6Beginner Design: The Standard§f:
§b• 1×§f Steam Channel
§b• 4×§f Fuel Channels (MEP-239 rods recommended)
§b• 8×§f Boron Neutron Absorbers
§b• 4×§f Graphite Moderators
§b• 1×§f Industrial Steam Turbine
§b• 1×§f Auxiliary Cooling Tower
§b• 18×§f Cover Panels
§b• 4×§f Autoloaders
§bOutput: §e3.75 MHE/s§f with MEP fuel

§6Heat Management§f:
• Boiler columns convert heat to steam — the primary cooling method
• Steam densities: higher = less cooling needed but more turbine stages
• Closed-loop cooling: condense steam back to water for 100% efficiency
• Cooler columns void heat but consume cryogel (50 mB/t)

§6Control Rods§f:
• Generally unnecessary for stable fuels (they reach equilibrium)
• Essential for highly reactive fuels to limit reactivity
• Use with extreme caution — one point of extension can flip a reactor from decaying to runaway

§6Reflectors§f:
• Great for low-power fuels but §cdeath sentence§f with balefire/digamma
• Always put a control rod between fuel and reflector for dangerous fuels

§6RBMK Fuel Recycling§f:
• Remove rods at ≥1% depletion when they stop contributing
• Cool rod below §e50°C§f (let sit in reactor or use Spent Fuel Pool Drum)
• Uncraft in 2×2 grid: 8 spent pellets + empty rod returned
• Process in §bSILEX§f (Laser Isotope Separation Chamber)
• Outputs are chance/proportion-based per depletion level
• 5 depletion increments: each 20% gives different product ratios
• Typically yields: unspent fuel + short-lived waste + long-lived waste
• Xenon-135 reduces other product yields if present

§6Depleted Fuel Guide§f:
• Hot depleted fuel from any reactor must be cooled in Spent Fuel Pool Drum
• Average cooling time: §e5 minutes§f (2s to 1h variance)
• Cooled fuel processes in Centrifuge: yields U-238, Pu nuggets, Tc-99, waste
• Automate with Thermal Dynamics pipes for safety

§ePro Tip§f: Use the §bRBMK Console§f to monitor average temp, depletion, and xenon across your reactor. The AZ-5 button is your emergency SCRAM!
